mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly CPI inflation above 2.5% signals central bank inaction, eroding real returns and causing S&P 500 forward declines over 20 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPI and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate earnings potential and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain SPX returns (R-squared < 0.15) in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates supply chain disruptions, reducing corporate margins and causing S&P 500 forward declines over 60 trading days as investors price in recession risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.5%, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns exceeds -0.1 in rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic demand, increasing corporate revenues and driving S&P 500 positive returns over 60 trading days through 2025.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform baseline returns in 60%+ of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, or the predictive power of GDPC1 drops below 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000k signals weak demand for construction, reducing related sector earnings and causing S&P 500 forward declines over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 1,000k, or the coefficient for HOUST in a regression model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a 'stagflation' risk, depressing equity valuations and causing S&P 500 forward declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to explain SPX returns (R-squared < 0.10) in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals labor market overheating, prompting central bank rate hikes that compress equity multiples and drive S&P 500 forward declines over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 5%, or the coefficient for UNRATE in a regression model is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
